text:
```json
[
  "An Operating System (OS) is software that acts as an interface between computer hardware components and the user.",
  "Every computer system must have at least one operating system to run other programs.",
  "The OS helps users communicate with the computer without knowing its language.",
  "The General Motors Research Lab implemented the first OS in the early 1950s for their IBM 701.",
  "Operating systems were first developed in the late 1950s to manage tape storage.",
  "In the mid-1960s, operating systems started to use disks.",
  "The first version of the Unix OS was developed in the late 1960s.",
  "Microsoft built its first OS, DOS, in 1981 by purchasing the 86-DOS software.",
  "The popular OS Windows first came into existence in 1985 when a GUI was created and paired with MS-DOS.",
  "Examples of operating systems include Windows (40.34% market share), Android (37.95%), iOS (15.44%), and Mac OS (4.34%).",
  "Popular types of OS include Batch, Multitasking/Time Sharing, Real Time, Distributed, Network, Multiprocessing, and Mobile Operating Systems.",
  "Batch operating systems group jobs with similar needs to run as a single batch, and users do not directly interact with the computer.",
  "Time-sharing operating systems allow multiple users at different terminals to use a single computer system simultaneously by sharing processor time.",
  "A real-time operating system has a very small time interval to process and respond to inputs, exemplified by Military and Space Software Systems.",
  "Distributed operating systems use many processors in different machines to provide fast computation.",
  "A Network Operating System runs on a server and manages data, users, groups, security, applications, and other networking functions.",
  "Mobile operating systems are designed to power smartphones, tablets, and wearable devices, with Android and iOS being famous examples.",
  "Key functions of an operating system include managing memory, files, processes, I/O systems, devices, security, communication, job accounting, networking, and command interpretation.",
  "Important features of an OS include information and resource protection, resource allocation, error detection and handling, file system manipulation, I/O operations, memory management, virtual memory, multitasking, program execution, disk access, device drivers, networking, security, and protected/supervisor mode.",
  "An OS acts as an intermediary between hardware and software, provides an easy-to-use format for system resources, offers an environment for program execution, and hides hardware details through abstraction.",
  "Disadvantages of an OS include potential security vulnerabilities, high software cost for small organizations, and the risk of losing stored content if an issue occurs.",
  "The kernel is the central component of a computer operating system, managing communication between software and hardware.",
  "Kernel features include context switching, process synchronization, inter-process communication, and low-level scheduling of processes.",
  "The two most popular types of kernels are Monolithic and Microkernels.",
  "A monolithic kernel is a single block of code that provides all required OS services and creates a distinct communication layer between hardware and software.",
  "A microkernel manages system resources with services implemented in different address spaces, storing user services in user space and kernel services in kernel space to reduce size.",
  "Firmware is programming embedded on a chip that controls a specific device, encoded by the manufacturer, and generally cannot be changed.",
  "An Operating System provides functionality beyond firmware, can be installed and changed by the user, and is stored on the hard drive.",
  "A 32-bit operating system processes 32 bits of data simultaneously and is limited to 3.2 GB of RAM.",
  "A 64-bit operating system processes 64 bits of data simultaneously and allows a maximum of 17 Billion GB of RAM."
]
```
json_data: An Operating System (OS) is software that acts as an interface between computer hardware components and the user.
Every computer system must have at least one operating system to run other programs.
The OS helps users communicate with the computer without knowing its language.
The General Motors Research Lab implemented the first OS in the early 1950s for their IBM 701.
Operating systems were first developed in the late 1950s to manage tape storage.
In the mid-1960s, operating systems started to use disks.
The first version of the Unix OS was developed in the late 1960s.
Microsoft built its first OS, DOS, in 1981 by purchasing the 86-DOS software.
The popular OS Windows first came into existence in 1985 when a GUI was created and paired with MS-DOS.
Examples of operating systems include Windows (40.34% market share), Android (37.95%), iOS (15.44%), and Mac OS (4.34%).
Popular types of OS include Batch, Multitasking/Time Sharing, Real Time, Distributed, Network, Multiprocessing, and Mobile Operating Systems.
Batch operating systems group jobs with similar needs to run as a single batch, and users do not directly interact with the computer.
Time-sharing operating systems allow multiple users at different terminals to use a single computer system simultaneously by sharing processor time.
A real-time operating system has a very small time interval to process and respond to inputs, exemplified by Military and Space Software Systems.
Distributed operating systems use many processors in different machines to provide fast computation.
A Network Operating System runs on a server and manages data, users, groups, security, applications, and other networking functions.
Mobile operating systems are designed to power smartphones, tablets, and wearable devices, with Android and iOS being famous examples.
Key functions of an operating system include managing memory, files, processes, I/O systems, devices, security, communication, job accounting, networking, and command interpretation.

Important features of an OS include information and resource protection, resource allocation, error detection and handling, file system manipulation, I/O operations, memory management, virtual memory, multitasking, program execution, disk access, device drivers, networking, security, and protected/supervisor mode.

An OS acts as an intermediary between hardware and software, provides an easy-to-use format for system resources, offers an environment for program execution, and hides hardware details through abstraction.
Disadvantages of an OS include potential security vulnerabilities, high software cost for small organizations, and the risk of losing stored content if an issue occurs.
The kernel is the central component of a computer operating system, managing communication between software and hardware.
Kernel features include context switching, process synchronization, inter-process communication, and low-level scheduling of processes.
The two most popular types of kernels are Monolithic and Microkernels.
A monolithic kernel is a single block of code that provides all required OS services and creates a distinct communication layer between hardware and software.
A microkernel manages system resources with services implemented in different address spaces, storing user services in user space and kernel services in kernel space to reduce size.
Firmware is programming embedded on a chip that controls a specific device, encoded by the manufacturer, and generally cannot be changed.
An Operating System provides functionality beyond firmware, can be installed and changed by the user, and is stored on the hard drive.
A 32-bit operating system processes 32 bits of data simultaneously and is limited to 3.2 GB of RAM.
A 64-bit operating system processes 64 bits of data simultaneously and allows a maximum of 17 Billion GB of RAM.
